title: Zerohash Becomes First to Secure Full EMI License Under Europe's MiCA
summary: Zerohash Europe is the first company to obtain both a MiCA license and full EMI status, allowing it to offer stablecoin and brokerage services across the EU. This milestone could set a precedent for other crypto firms operating in Europe.
body:
Zerohash Europe has made history by becoming the first firm to secure a full Electronic Money Institution (EMI) license under Europe’s Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) regulation. This dual licensing allows the company to provide stablecoin and brokerage services across the European Union, marking a significant step in the region’s crypto regulatory framework.

The MiCA license, combined with the EMI status, enables Zerohash to offer a broader range of financial services, including the issuance and management of stablecoins, as well as brokerage services for crypto assets. This move is expected to enhance consumer trust and operational transparency within the European crypto market.

For everyday users, this development means increased access to regulated and secure crypto services. The EMI license ensures that Zerohash adheres to stringent financial regulations, providing a safer environment for users to engage in stablecoin transactions and brokerage services. This could also encourage other crypto firms to seek similar licenses, fostering a more robust and compliant crypto ecosystem in Europe.

As Zerohash begins to leverage its new licenses, users can expect a more seamless and secure experience when using its stablecoin and brokerage services. This milestone could also pave the way for other companies to follow suit, potentially leading to a more regulated and trustworthy crypto market in Europe. Watch for more firms to apply for similar licenses in the coming months.
